signals.rationale: Exxon is a big energy company and people always need energy. With moderate inflation, energy prices might go up, which is good for Exxon.
Pfizer is a solid healthcare company with a good dividend. High interest rates might make investors look for safer options, so it's okay to hold but not a great time to buy.
Verizon is a big telecom company and people always need phone service. It also pays a good dividend, which is attractive when interest rates are high.
UnitedHealth is a big healthcare company, but high interest rates might make investors less excited about expensive stocks like this one. Maybe look elsewhere for now.
Bank of America is a big bank and high interest rates can be good for banks. But it's not a great time to buy more because the economy is a bit uncertain.
Chevron is another big energy company and energy prices might go up with moderate inflation. It also pays a good dividend, which is attractive to investors.
Walmart is a big retailer and people always need to buy stuff. But high interest rates might make investors less excited about stocks that aren't growing super fast.
Johnson & Johnson is a solid healthcare company with a good track record. It's a relatively safe option when the economy is a bit uncertain.
MetLife is an insurance company and people always need insurance. It also pays a good dividend, which is attractive to investors when interest rates are high.
Tesla is a expensive stock and high interest rates might make investors less excited about expensive stocks. It's also a bit of a risky option, so maybe look elsewhere for now.
